summary:
Shorter treatment durations using hypofracitonation or stereotactic radiosurgery do not worsen oncologic outcomes in prostate cancer patients; they provide equivalent tumor control while minimizing toxicity. Evidence from multiple studies confirms these intensified protocols are effective and safe, especially when compared to traditional longer treatments.

Only two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically significant findings:

Shorter radiation therapy durations, such as those used in hypofractionated or stereotactic schemes, do not impair tumor control or increase toxicity in prostate cancer. Clinical data support their equivalence to standard-dose therapies in terms of both efficacy and safety.
